highlights.title: AI coding tools may be the end of freemium utility apps
highlights.description:
With the recent launch of tools like OpenAI’s Codex, Anthropic’s Claude Agent, and the upcoming support for these tools right in Xcode, the market for single-purpose apps might not be long for this world. Here’s why.
more…

highlights.title: Apple Announces Plans to Begin Assembling Mac Mini in U.S. This Year
highlights.description:
Apple today announced that Foxconn will begin assembling some Mac mini computers at a factory in Houston, Texas later this year.


In an interview with The Wall Street Journal, Apple's operations chief Sabih Khan said U.S. assembly of some Mac mini units is part of the company's previously-announced commitment to invest $600 billion in the U.S. by August 2029.

Mac mini units assembled in the U.S. will primarily serve the U.S. market, while production will continue in Asia for orders abroad, according to Khan.

received its first major redesign since 2010, along with M4 and M4 Pro chips.

In the U.S., the Mac mini starts at just $599, but customers must supply their own display, keyboard, and trackpad or mouse.

in 2019, during U.S. President Donald Trump's first term, and the company's plan to begin assembling some Mac mini units in the U.S. comes during Trump's second term.

The Wall Street Journal also explored Apple's efforts to boost its U.S. chip production.



Apple plans to purchase more than 100 million chips from TSMC's Arizona factory this year, according to the company's global head of procurement David Tom.
Related Roundup: Mac mini
Tag: The Wall Street Journal
Buyer's Guide: Mac Mini (Caution)
Related Forum: Mac mini
This article, "Apple Announces Plans to Begin Assembling Mac Mini in U.S. This Year" first appeared on MacRumors.com

highlights.title: Microsoft teases ‘reimagined SharePoint experience’ landing in April
Anthropic accuses China's AI labs of ripping off content - just like it did
Stripe, PayPal Ventures bet on India’s Xflow to fix cross-border B2B payments
highlights.description: Stripe and PayPal Ventures have participated in Xflow's $16.6 million round that gives it a post-money valuation of $85 million.

highlights.title: Tesla’s battle with the California Department of Motor Vehicles isn’t over after all
highlights.description: Tesla has filed a lawsuit against the California DMV in the ongoing battle around Autopilot.

highlights.title: A Meta AI security researcher said an OpenClaw agent ran amok on her inbox
highlights.description: The viral X post from an AI security researcher reads like satire. But it's really a word of warning about what can go wrong when handing tasks to an AI agent.

highlights.title: Citrini’s Dystopia Fuels Asia’s Breakaway From Global AI Gloom
highlights.description: Citrini Research’s vision of a dystopian future shaped by artificial intelligence is reinforcing bets that Asia will emerge as a winner of the AI disruption, thanks to its wealth of companies like chipmakers, in contrast to the turmoil hitting parts of the US market.

highlights.title: Uber Buys Parking App SpotHero to Grow Its Customer Base
highlights.description: Uber Technologies Inc. is buying parking reservation app SpotHero Inc., the latest move by the rideshare company to expand its customer base.

highlights.title: Indian IT Stock Selloff Deepens on AI Scare After Citrini Report
highlights.description: A rout in Indian software services stocks deepened after a report from Citrini Research amplified worries about businesses that are vulnerable to the growing influence of artificial intelligence.

ai.title: Sagard's Desmarais: Bullish on Software, Tech & AI's Impact
ai.description: Paul Desmarais III, chairman, CEO and co-founder of Sagard, says AI is one of the firm’s three strategic pillars. He tells Romaine Bostick and Kristine Aquino on "The Close" that AI represents a significant opportunity for industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

ai.title: Apple’s new ‘Sales Coach’ app is now live on the App Store, replacing ‘SEED’
ai.description:
Following last week’s report that Apple was preparing to replace its “SEED” app with “Sales Coach,” the new app is now available. Here are the details.
